codeReview.summary: Manual scrutiny review confirmed the P2P packet addressed the actual PR #98 blockers: replay-first supplier settlement/accounting behavior was restored, R2 evidence landed with the code changes, the packet-local docs nit was fixed, and the refreshed changed-files coverage logic was covered by dedicated CI contract tests before the branch fast gate was rerun successfully. The review now anchors to branch-reachable commit `7b442c757a8cb4c55809d6235c56de18777506a0` (`fix(p2p): close PR #98 changed coverage gate`) so the shareout packet reports current-stack reachability truthfully.

issues.description: The worker noted that running the full `python3 -m unittest testing.ci.test_pr_review_ci_packet` module still fails `RuntimeProbeContractTest.test_runtime_probe_fails_closed_on_health_status` because `.factory/services.yaml` does not yet match that runtime-probe expectation on this branch.
issues.suggestedFix: Resolve the runtime probe contract in the packet that owns `.factory/services.yaml` healthcheck semantics, then rerun the full CI packet unittest module if that contract becomes part of the required validator surface.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The full CI packet unittest module still contains a runtime-probe contract expectation for a 9090 actuator healthcheck sequence that does not match the current backend-compose-v2 healthcheck in `.factory/services.yaml`, even though the required recovery validators for this packet passed.

summary: Pass with non-blocking follow-up. PR #98 replay/governance/coverage blockers were closed and validated on branch-reachable commit `7b442c757a8cb4c55809d6235c56de18777506a0`, but a broader CI contract test still documents a runtime probe mismatch outside the required packet gates.
